svg.text: Conv_Simple / CV-101 (garage conveyor) — device schedule · sheet index · wire-numbering key · line-style legend
Conveyor controller; Modbus RTU master to VFD1
Drives conveyor motor; commanded over Modbus RTU (Ch2, node 1)
Conveyor drive motor (3~ from VFD1 U/V/W)
Emergency stop, DUAL-CHANNEL (diverse) — NC ch to I-02, NO ch to I-03
Direction selector — FWD contact to I-00, REV contact to I-01 (FWD-OFF-REV)
Run / rearm pushbutton to I-04; illuminated — lamp fed by PLC O-03 (PBRunLED)
Product-present beam to I-05 (blocked -> pe_latched soft stop)
24 VDC control supply (documented on E-004); feeds +24V/0V to input loop
Upstream branch protection (E-002/E-003) — existence, type, rating unconfirmed
Safety power contactor — coil driven by PLC O-02 ('ContactorQ1', safety power...
Green RUNNING pilot — load of O-00 (LightGreen)
Red FAULT/E-STOP pilot — load of O-01 (LightRed)
Field terminal strip — field devices land here before PLC (E-008)
Conductor numbers are W[sheet-digit][line-2d] (e.g. W503 = sheet E-005, line 3) — all PROPOSED until as-built (OI-03/OI-19). Rail/bus
labels (W24, W0V) are exempt. Comms sheets (E-007) use mnemonic conductor labels (485+/485-/SGND/SH) per CommsToVFD ancestry. Same
electrical node = same wire number; a number changes only through a device.
• LOTO + wait ≥5 min for DC-bus discharge before touching (WI p.2 §2).
• Monitored e-stop is NOT a safety stop — hardwire S0 to remove drive power per NFPA 79 / EN 60204-1 (style law §7;
